text: This past winter, when I was in Berlin for grad school...
I constantly had family and friends texting to ask, 'How was Europe?'

notification.title: Mom
notification.message: How is Switzerland? Sweden? I wish we could make the trip

text: The typical response for something like that is to just say, 'Wow, I'm having such a fun time.'
Even when that's not the case.
And I know that this realization isn't unique to me.
But when I came back from Berlin, I started looking at my data.
And I realized that Meta, Google, TikTok knew so much about me.

notification.title: Google
notification.message: You're watching YouTube until 4:00 AM again.

text: They knew what hours I was doomscrolling...

notification.title: Instagram
notification.message: You are lonelier than normal. Here's ads for gambling websites.

text: The divisive topics that crept into my late night scroll sessions.

notification.title: TikTok
notification.message: You really should be getting some sleep right now.

text: And they knew much more about my actual mental state than the people that are supposed to be closest to me.
And since then, I've thought about...
What if every time someone asks 'How are you doing?', 'How are you?', 'Are you enjoying your travels?'
I took the time to actually dig deep and answer honestly.
I want you to go to your message app, search 'How are you', and type in what you responded to that question.
